title: Database Connection Pool Tuning Guide — Exhaustion Prevention
content:
Database Connection Pool Tuning Guide

Context: Payment processing outages in March 2024 were traced to PostgreSQL connection pool saturation under peak load. This guide prevents recurrence.

Symptoms of Pool Exhaustion:
- HikariCP 'Connection is not available, request timed out' errors in payments-api logs.
- Rising active connection count at pool max_size ceiling.
- Increasing queue wait time (> 500ms) on pool borrow.
- Correlated P95 payment latency spikes.

Recommended Settings (payments-api, per pod):
- maximumPoolSize: 20 (default 10 — increase only after DBA review)
- minimumIdle: 5
- connectionTimeout: 30000 ms
- idleTimeout: 600000 ms
- maxLifetime: 1800000 ms
- leakDetectionThreshold: 60000 ms (staging only)

Prevention Checklist:
1. Ensure all DB access uses connection pool; never open raw connections in request handlers.
2. Keep transactions short; avoid long-running queries inside payment authorization path.
3. Scale horizontally (more pods) before raising per-pod pool max — total connections must stay under PostgreSQL max_connections budget.
4. Enable pool metrics export to Prometheus; alert when utilization > 80% for 5 minutes.
5. Load-test peak scenarios (Black Friday simulation) before major releases.

Emergency Mitigation (during outage):
- Scale payments-api replicas +2 to spread load.
- Temporarily increase maximumPoolSize by 25% with DBA approval.
- Kill idle-in-transaction sessions older than 5 minutes (runbook script: db-kill-idle-tx.sh).
- If saturation persists, initiate read-replica failover for read-heavy paths only.

Capacity Formula:
- Total connections = pods × maximumPoolSize + admin overhead (reserve 10%).
- Coordinate with Platform DBA before changing cluster-wide limits.

Related: Payment Outage Runbook (recovery section), incident INC-2024-0318 postmortem.
